description: New user with complex tax profile — first time using the skill
user_message: I need to start my 2025 tax prep. I'm married filing jointly, we have two kids (ages 4 and 7), I have a W-2 from Acme Corp and 1099-NEC income from my consulting business, three rental properties in NJ and NY, brokerage accounts at two major firms, and K-1s from two angel investments. I use Monarch to track everything. Help me get organized for my CPA.
expected_behavior: Skill should: (1) create ~/.tax-prep/2025/ directory, (2) conduct situation interview one question at a time, building on provided info, (3) pull current-year IRS rates, (4) create profile.json, (5) transition to Phase 2 and map accounts to expected documents using document-matrix, (6) generate accounts.json with ~20+ expected documents

description: Returning user with prior year data — property sold
user_message: Let's do tax prep for 2025. I did this last year too. One change: I sold one of my rental properties this year.
expected_behavior: Skill should: (1) load prior year profile.json, (2) present summary of last year's situation, (3) ask 'what changed' rather than re-interviewing from scratch, (4) handle the sold property — ask for closing statement details, (5) reference schedule-e-guide.md for sale/depreciation recapture, (6) update profile with property disposition

description: User uploads a PDF document during an active session
user_message: I just got my 1099-DIV from First National Brokerage. Here it is.
expected_behavior: Skill should: (1) read the PDF, (2) identify it as 1099-DIV, (3) extract key fields (ordinary dividends, qualified dividends, capital gains distributions, foreign tax paid), (4) present extracted values to user for confirmation, (5) match to expected document in accounts.json, (6) update status to received, (7) report overall progress

description: Self-filer wanting TurboTax export instead of CPA package
user_message: I don't use a CPA. Can you help me organize my tax docs and export them so I can import into TurboTax?
expected_behavior: Skill should: (1) proceed through normal workflow, (2) in Phase 5 generate TXF file using txf-field-mapping.md, (3) read back the TXF file and verify values, (4) provide import instructions for TurboTax, (5) include disclaimer about reviewing imported data, (6) note limitations of TXF format

description: User has uploaded all docs and provides Monarch CSV for reconciliation
user_message: I've uploaded all my docs. Here's my Monarch export too. Can you check everything matches?
expected_behavior: Skill should: (1) read Monarch CSV, (2) compare interest income in 1099-INTs against Monarch interest transactions, (3) compare dividend income against Monarch dividends, (4) flag discrepancies > $50, (5) run completeness check against accounts.json, (6) load common-gaps.md and ask about commonly missed items for user's profile, (7) generate reconciliation.json

description: User uploads a document during Phase 1 interview — tests fluid phase handling
user_message: I'm married filing jointly with two kids. Oh wait, I just got my W-2 from Acme Corp — here it is. [attaches PDF]
expected_behavior: Skill should: (1) process the W-2 immediately without insisting on completing the interview first, (2) extract W-2 fields and confirm with user, (3) save to state, (4) resume the interview where it left off, (5) report progress including the processed document

description: Generate CPA package and verify all required sections and safety elements
user_message: All my documents are in. Generate the CPA package.
expected_behavior: Skill should: (1) generate cpa-package.md with all 11 sections, (2) include cover letter with filing status and document count, (3) include tax estimate with disclaimer containing 'NOT tax advice', (4) show 'TBD' for any missing document amounts instead of $0, (5) include open questions from session-notes.json, (6) include year-over-year comparison if prior year data exists

description: User corrects a previously entered value — tests session notes
user_message: Actually, I need to correct something. My Q4 federal estimated payment was $4,000 not $3,000. I just checked my bank records.
expected_behavior: Skill should: (1) update the value in the appropriate state file, (2) append a correction entry to session-notes.json with previous_value and new_value, (3) confirm the change back to the user, (4) note this will be reflected in the CPA package
